summary: Jill presents a fascinating contradiction - a qualified dietitian who relies heavily on food delivery despite her professional knowledge. At 30+, she's juggling full-time sustainability work with a postgraduate course, creating intense time pressure that drives her toward convenience solutions. Her communication style is thoughtful and self-aware, often catching herself mid-sentence to provide more accurate information. She demonstrates sophisticated understanding of nutrition misinformation on social media, yet admits her own eating habits have deteriorated over time. Her relationship with food delivery is pragmatic rather than indulgent - she sees it as a necessary tool for managing her busy schedule, particularly on college evenings when she's 'online from 9 to 9.' She shows remarkable self-regulation by cancelling her Deliveroo Plus subscription when she realized it was encouraging overconsumption, suggesting she actively manages her own behavioral triggers. Her food choices are influenced by location convenience, nutritional considerations (adding vegetables to orders), and social recommendations from friends and family.
personality_notes: Measured, self-reflective communicator who frequently self-corrects. Shows high self-awareness about her contradictions and behavioral patterns. Professional yet personable, with a tendency toward detailed explanations.
food_relationship: Views food delivery as a practical necessity rather than indulgence, driven by time constraints despite professional nutrition knowledge.

key_moments.description: Reveals she's a dietitian while discussing her heavy reliance on takeaway
key_moments.significance: Core contradiction that defines her relationship with food - professional knowledge vs. practical behavior

key_moments.description: Admits cancelling Deliveroo Plus because it encouraged too much ordering
key_moments.significance: Shows sophisticated self-awareness and active management of her own consumption triggers

key_moments.description: Detailed explanation of dinner stress on college evenings
key_moments.significance: Reveals the genuine time pressure and decision fatigue that drives delivery usage

what_not_said: Never discussed guilt about professional vs. personal food choices
Avoided mentioning specific dietary restrictions despite being vegetarian
Did not elaborate on why her healthy takeaway habits deteriorated
initial_hypotheses: Professional food knowledge may create internal conflict but doesn't override convenience needs
Time scarcity is the primary driver, not laziness or lack of cooking skills
She may represent a segment of health-conscious professionals who use delivery strategically
Social influence (friends, family) plays a significant role in her platform and restaurant choices
questions_for_coding: How do health professionals reconcile their knowledge with convenience behaviors?
What role does time pressure play versus other factors in delivery decisions?
How does professional identity influence food delivery platform preferences?
What triggers self-regulation behaviors in heavy delivery users?
